title: The Ferry to the Island
synopsis: A short ferry trip feels like a big adventure to a child crossing open water for the first time.
sentences: Toby had never been on a ferry before.
He held the rail tightly as the boat pulled away from the harbour.
The wind smelled of salt, and white birds followed the ferry through the air.
Toby looked back and watched the town grow smaller behind them.
His mother pointed to the island waiting ahead in the distance.
The water moved in long grey lines under the boat.
At one moment, the ferry rose and fell enough to make Toby laugh.
A crew member smiled and said the sea was calm that day.
Inside the cabin, Toby drank orange juice and pressed his nose to the glass.
Soon the island came closer, with green hills and a small line of houses.
When the ferry reached the dock, Toby stepped off feeling brave.
The trip had been short, but to him it had felt enormous.
